steps.title: Spot the relationship
steps.content: Sentence 1 says metals do not maintain bonds by sharing and exchanging electrons. Sentence 2 describes what actually happens instead: valence electrons travel freely. The blank must signal a correction — Sentence 2 replaces the denied mechanism with the real one.

steps.title: Eliminate and confirm
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘For example,’: signals that Sentence 2 is a specific instance of Sentence 1 — but Sentence 1 is a negation, and Sentence 2 contradicts it rather than illustrating it. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘Rather,’: signals "instead" — pivoting from the denied mechanism to the actual one. ✓
  • Choice C — ‘Accordingly,’: signals cause-and-effect — Sentence 2 is not a result of the negation in Sentence 1. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘Likewise,’: signals similarity — Sentence 2 is the opposite of what Sentence 1 describes, not a parallel case. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
